2606.19710.title: FineREX: Fine-Tuned NER-RE for Human Smuggling Knowledge Graphs
2606.19710.paper_summary: Court proceedings contain valuable evidence about human smuggling networks, but this information is often buried within unstructured, jargon-heavy legal documents. While large language models (LLMs) can support knowledge graph construction through automated information extraction, existing approaches rely on general-purpose models that are not tailored to the entity and relationship definitions required in this domain. We introduce FineREX, a streamlined knowledge graph construction pipeline built around a fine-tuned LLM for named entity recognition and relationship extraction (NER-RE). Using a manually annotated dataset of $512$ text chunks, FineREX achieves absolute improvements of 15.50% and 31.46% in entity and relationship F1-score, respectively, compared to a larger general-purpose baseline. These gains translate into higher-quality knowledge graphs, reducing legal noise by nearly half and lowering node duplication on long documents from 17.78% to 11.17%. By eliminating document rewriting and redundant extraction stages, FineREX also reduces end-to-end processing time by 50.0%. Our results demonstrate that domain-specific fine-tuning can substantially outperform larger general-purpose models while improving both the quality and efficiency of knowledge graph construction for illicit network analysis.
2606.19710.paper_summary_zh: 法院程序包含有關人類走私網絡的寶貴證據，但這些信息通常埋藏在結構不清、術語繁多的法律文件中。雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）可以通過自動信息提取來支持知識圖譜的構建，但現有的方法依賴於不針對該領域所需的實體和關係定義的通用模型。我們介紹了FineREX，一個圍繞經過微調的LLM構建的精簡知識圖譜構建流程，用於命名實體識別和關係提取（NER-RE）。使用一個手動標註的$512$個文本片段的數據集，FineREX在實體和關係F1分數上分別實現了15.50%和31.46%的絕對提升，相較於一個更大的通用基準。這些增益轉化為更高質量的知識圖譜，將法律噪音減少近一半，並將長文檔中的節點重複率從17.78%降低到11.17%。通過消除文檔重寫和冗餘提取階段，FineREX還將端到端處理時間減少了50.0%。我們的結果表明，特定領域的微調可以顯著超越更大的通用模型，同時提高非法網絡分析的知識圖譜構建的質量和效率。
2606.19710.author: Elijah Feldman et.al.
2606.19710.authors: Elijah Feldman, Dipak Meher, Carlotta Domeniconi
